---
title: Positron Server available for academic use via JupyterHub
subtitle: Academic institutions can now host Positron Server for their students at **no cost**
author: Isabel Zimmerman
date: "2026/04/03"
---

Positron Server is now available on JupyterHub.

If your institution already runs JupyterHub, you can offer Positron as a launcher option alongside JupyterLab or classic notebooks. 
No additional infrastructure required!

With a free teaching license, institutions can provide Positron Server to currently enrolled students for educational use in connection with coursework.

Positron Server includes the same features as the desktop version, including rich Python and R support, access to the OpenVSX extension marketplace, Positron Assistant, and more.

## Who is this for?

This offering is designed for academic institutions using Positron as a teaching tool.

Eligible institutions can host Positron Server for enrolled students, course participants, and instructional staff under a free teaching license.

Full eligibility details are available in the [Positron Education License Rider](https://github.com/posit-dev/positron/blob/main/LICENSE.txt).

## How it works

For students, getting started is simple: log into JupyterHub and select Positron from the launcher.
No downloads or setup required.

For administrators, Positron Server is integrated into JupyterHub via the [`jupyter-positron-server` Python package](https://github.com/posit-dev/jupyter-positron-server), which is built on Jupyter Server Proxy. 
If you’ve configured server proxies before, the setup will feel familiar.
Setup instructions are available in the [package documentation](https://posit-dev.github.io/jupyter-positron-server/).

This is not a standalone desktop installation. 
Instead, it integrates Positron directly into your existing JupyterHub environment.
Positron Server is compatible with JupyterHub environments running JupyterLab 4 and Python 3.9+.

## Getting a license

To host Positron Server for teaching purposes, you’ll need a free license key:

1. Review the eligibility criteria in the [Positron Education License Rider](https://github.com/posit-dev/positron/blob/main/LICENSE.txt).
2. Email [academic-licenses@posit.co](mailto:academic-licenses@posit.co) to express interest in using Positron for teaching.
3. Once approved, follow the [`jupyter-positron-server` documentation](https://posit-dev.github.io/jupyter-positron-server/) to complete setup.

---

Have questions or want to learn more? 

Email [academic-licenses@posit.co](mailto:academic-licenses@posit.co) and mention that you're interested in Positron. 
We're happy to help you figure out next steps for your institution.
